nature: control-plane only; the active first attempt was never touched; no driver, task, row, manifest, threshold, verifier, sampling, cap, or gate changed

corrections.owned_process_group: a minimal launcher (chdir + setsid + exec) makes the recorded PID the exact owned PGID; stall signals go only to that PGID after re-proving its cmdline and cwd; host-global pgrep/pkill/name-matching is gone; the superseded bash supervisor now hard-refuses execution
corrections.attempt_accounting: a persisted private launch record (captured live from the running original attempt: PID==PGID, cmdline, cwd, python, vllm/torch versions, protocol env) counts the original launch as attempt one; the controller permits at most one additional launch and blocks permanently once the budget is spent
corrections.single_writer: an exclusive nonblocking flock keyed to the row-bound run id is acquired before preflight and held for the retry lifetime; liveness of any recorded driver for the run identity (exact cmdline+cwd proof, PID-reuse safe) blocks launch
corrections.runtime_identity: retry verifies the original python executable, vllm and torch versions, protocol environment variables, and byte-equal model pointers between control and execution checkouts against the private launch record; missing evidence blocks; immutable-source + exact-ledger preflight runs after the lock and immediately before engine construction
corrections.crash_consistent_budget: attempt two is durably consumed BEFORE process creation: the private record is atomically replaced (temp + os.replace + fsync of file and parent directory) in state reserved with a unique nonce; any attempt-two trace (reserved/launching/running/exited), including reserved-without-PID after a crash, blocks every later launch permanently and is never refunded; the record is atomically advanced to running with the owned PID/PGID only after the exec identity proof
corrections.model_identity_bound_to_original: the original launch record (captured live from attempt one) now carries the model-pointer sha256; the control pointer, execution pointer, AND the actual retry launch argument must all match that recorded digest — two current files agreeing is insufficient; missing original evidence blocks
corrections.ambiguous_process_blocks: proc identity distinguishes proven-nonexistent (dead) from unreadable/partial /proc evidence (AmbiguousProcess -> block); a recorded PID with unreadable identity can never be treated as dead
corrections.protocol_env_schema: a frozen five-key protocol environment schema is defined in code and mirrored in the private record (values or explicit nulls); the retry environment sets recorded values exactly and explicitly removes recorded absences so ambient values cannot leak; missing schema evidence blocks
corrections.durable_process_ownership: a barrier handshake closes the unguarded launch window: the child setsids then BLOCKS on an inherited pipe and can only exec the scientific driver after the controller durably writes a nonce-bound launching record with the exact PID/PGID/expected-command/exec-dir/source/launcher+preflight+environment digests, rereads and exact-validates it, and releases the barrier; parent death before release means pipe EOF and the child exits without exec and without ledger contact; exec-timeout and post-creation exceptions terminate and reap only the exact owned group (or record a permanent ambiguous block when ownership cannot be proved) and durably record the terminal state — no failure path leaves a live unrecorded child
corrections.lock_survival: the run-ID flock descriptor is inherited through exec, so a controller crash after barrier release leaves the lock held by the surviving driver; the lock releases only when the driver exits
corrections.pinned_control_artifacts: the launcher and preflight bytes are pinned by sha256 in the private record and reverified immediately before process creation; any control-plane edit blocks a retry even when the immutable scientific checkout and ledger are unchanged
corrections.complete_environment_envelope: attempt one's COMPLETE process environment (77 variables) was captured privately as exact bytes with a digest while its /proc identity was proven; a retry environment is reconstructed solely from those recorded bytes — ambient os.environ is never inherited; missing, unreadable, or digest-mismatched evidence blocks
corrections.no_explicit_unlock: the controller never calls LOCK_UN after the lock descriptor is inherited; it closes only its own duplicate, so a surviving child keeps the run identity locked until its last descriptor closes (source-asserted by test)
corrections.strict_termination_only: permissive termination is gone: full ownership evidence (PID/PGID/SID, /proc stat start time, command phase, cwd) is captured while the child is behind the barrier and durably bound; ownership is re-proved immediately before BOTH the TERM and KILL signals with pidfd PID-reuse resistance where supported; any mismatch or unreadable field is a permanent ambiguous block with no signal
corrections.fd_allowlist: launches use close_fds=True with an exact pass-fd allowlist (barrier + run lock); the guarded launcher audits /proc/self/fd pre-exec and aborts (rc 4, no exec, no ledger contact) on any unexpected descriptor; the barrier closes before exec
corrections.probes_under_original_identity: the complete recorded environment is reconstructed and digest-verified BEFORE any package-dependent check; vllm/torch probes run under the original executable (bound by the sha256 captured from /proc/<pid>/exe while attempt one was alive) and the recorded environment, comparing version and module origin; the controller's ambient interpreter orchestrates file I/O only
corrections.untracked_import_audit: a read-only audit of attempt one's execution tree found 6 untracked paths, zero importable or executable by the driver, zero symlinks in import roots (aggregate PASS recorded privately); retry preflights and finalization require the all-untracked audit with an explicit private non-importable allowlist
corrections.preflight_under_bound_identity: the retry order is: load+validate record under lock → reconstruct+digest-verify the complete environment → bind the executable identity → run the Python-importing preflight AND all package probes under that exact executable and environment (never sys.executable/ambient) → reverify source/artifacts/model/ledger/executable immediately before reservation and process creation
corrections.executable_binding: the executable is bound by canonical path, device, inode, mode, size, and sha256 captured read-only while attempt one was alive; the invoked pathname must resolve to that exact identity; the preflight, probes, guarded launcher, and driver are all invoked by the bound executable; the launcher re-proves the sha behind the barrier immediately before scientific exec (aborts rc 5 on any change)
corrections.package_origin_comparison: vllm/torch (and locally imported packages) are compared on version AND canonical origin AND origin-file sha256 under the bound executable and recorded environment — same-version/different-origin resolution blocks (test-proven)
corrections.fail_closed_liveness: driver_alive is fail-closed: proven nonexistence or a different /proc start time (PID reuse) is dead; same start time with any cmdline/cwd/SID/PGID/phase mismatch, unreadable evidence, or missing recorded fields raises AmbiguousProcess and blocks; only exact full identity counts as alive
corrections.reusable_untracked_audit: scripts/m38e_untracked_audit.py enumerates all untracked files/dirs/symlinks, derives import+execution roots from the recorded environment and bound executable, and blocks importable/executable/symlinked/.pth/startup-hook/editable-install artifacts (only the explicit private operational allowlist outside roots passes); run by both retry preflight and finalization; attempt-1 tree audited clean
corrections.pidfd_termination: termination signals the leader through its pidfd (PID-reuse immune) and each session descendant only through its own freshly opened pidfd after per-process proof — never a bare killpg after leader identity is unavailable; platforms without pidfd_send_signal fail closed (ambiguous, no auto-termination)
corrections.ambiguous_stall_no_hang: an ambiguous stall termination durably records a permanent 'ambiguous' block, does NOT call an unbounded wait, closes only the controller's lock duplicate (leaving a surviving child its inherited lock), and returns blocked for private operator review
corrections.two_way_handshake: the one-way barrier is replaced by a READY/GO handshake: the child writes its self-observed pid/pgid/sid/cwd and /proc/self/exe identity after chdir+setsid+FD-audit, then blocks; the parent verifies (with a bounded timeout) exact PID=PGID=SID, cwd == the immutable execution dir, launcher command phase, kernel start time, and executable identity against the kernel view before durably registering ownership and sending GO; EOF/timeout/mismatch/parent-death exits the child without exec
corrections.behind_barrier_exe_reproof: the launcher re-proves BOTH the running interpreter (/proc/self/exe) and the target executable by canonical path, device, inode, mode, size, and sha256 immediately before exec, and exec's the canonical resolved path (not the invoked alias); alias/symlink/inode-replacement/wrapper changes abort rc 5
corrections.full_identity_liveness: driver_alive now compares kernel start time, canonical executable, SID, PGID, argv, and cwd; only proven nonexistence or a different start time (PID reuse) is dead; any same-start mismatch, unreadable field, or missing recorded field is ambiguous and blocks
corrections.hardened_audit: the untracked/import audit is pinned in control_artifact_sha256 and verified with the launcher/preflight; it enforces git return codes, parses NUL-delimited output, enumerates untracked AND ignored files, derives Python import + PATH execution roots under the recorded environment, binds the git executable by identity, uses exact-path/component allowlists, and fails closed on any command failure, undecodable name, permission error, or symlink issue; the in-repo venv is excluded as closure-bound and bytecode caches of tracked sources are skipped
corrections.complete_dependency_closure: attempt one's full import closure (11 execution modules: driver, families, capture, worker-ext, verifier, torch, vllm, numpy, transformers, sklearn) was captured with per-module canonical origin + sha256 while alive; the retry verifies every module origin and digest under the bound executable and recorded environment
corrections.fail_closed_termination: no trustworthy cgroup/systemd scope is bindable from pure Python, so automatic stall termination is DISABLED and fail-closed: a stall durably records a permanent 'stall_ambiguous' block and returns without signaling, without an unbounded wait, and without releasing a surviving child's inherited lock — private operator review required (the steer's explicit escape hatch)

tests: tests/test_m38e_retry_controller.py — 31 synthetic-process tests covering the READY/GO handshake, behind-barrier exe abort, full-identity liveness (start-time/exe/SID/PGID/cwd/argv), PID-reuse-is-dead, hardened audit (clean pass + planted-shadow block via bound git and NUL parsing), inherited-lock survival, pidfd leader termination, and the fail-closed stall block
privacy_check_status: aggregate amendment only; PIDs/paths/env stay in the private record
